hallOfFame.name: Datuk Yee Moh Chai
hallOfFame.bio: A pioneering Kadazan political figure who championed indigenous rights and the formalization of the KDM identity in Sabah. Served multiple terms as a state assemblyman and was instrumental in cultural preservation efforts.

hallOfFame.name: Cleophas Mojuntin
hallOfFame.bio: Known as 'The Undying Eagle of Penampang,' Mojuntin was a founding father of Sabah politics and one of the most respected KDM leaders. He died tragically in the Rajah Brooke aircraft disaster in 1976.

hallOfFame.name: Judeth Justin Anad
hallOfFame.bio: A master of the sompoton and tongkungon, Judeth has dedicated her life to preserving traditional Kadazan Dusun musical heritage. She has performed at national and international folk festivals, bringing KDM music to global audiences.

hallOfFame.name: Arnold Panajou
hallOfFame.bio: One of Sabah's greatest long-distance runners, Arnold represented Malaysia at multiple SEA Games events. His achievements brought pride to the Murut community and paved the way for generations of KDM athletes.

hallOfFame.name: Dorina Ginsigau
hallOfFame.bio: A celebrated Rungus traditional dancer and costume designer who has worked to preserve authentic Rungus sumazau choreography and the intricate manik (beadwork) traditions of Northern Sabah.

hallOfFame.name: Dr. Bernard Godron Disimon
hallOfFame.bio: A linguistics professor at Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Dr. Disimon has published extensively on Kadazan-Dusun dialects and co-authored the first comprehensive Kadazan-Dusun dictionary for academic use.

hallOfFame.name: Richard Mojikon
hallOfFame.bio: A world-class silat practitioner who has won gold at multiple international championships. Richard is known for blending traditional Kadazan warrior philosophy with competitive silat, inspiring a new generation of KDM martial artists.

hallOfFame.name: Dayang Nurfaizah
hallOfFame.bio: One of Malaysia's most celebrated vocalists with Kadazan-Dusun roots, Dayang Nurfaizah has sold millions of albums nationally while proudly representing her heritage on the biggest stages in the country.

hallOfFame.name: Joniston Bangkuai
hallOfFame.bio: A committed servant of the Dusun people in the Mount Kinabalu highlands, Joniston has advocated tirelessly for eco-tourism rights, indigenous land protection, and rural community development in the Ranau district.
achievements: Mount Kinabalu Eco-Tourism Task Force chair

hallOfFame.name: Felicia Chin Juat Woan
hallOfFame.bio: Founder of Tambunan Tapai Co-op, Felicia has commercialized traditional rice wine (tapai) using sustainable fermentation methods, creating livelihoods for over 200 KDM families while preserving a centuries-old tradition.

hallOfFame.name: Sirinah Bondok
hallOfFame.bio: A master weaver of the traditional Rungus linangkit and tuhau cloth, Sirinah's intricate patterns have been exhibited at the National Museum of Malaysia and acquired by collectors across Southeast Asia.

hallOfFame.name: Vincent Pung
hallOfFame.bio: A stalwart midfielder for Sabah FA, Vincent captained the team during their historic return to the Malaysia Super League, becoming one of the most beloved footballers among the KDM community.

businesses.name: Tambunan Tapai Co-op
businesses.description: Artisan producers of premium traditional tapai (rice wine) using generations-old fermentation recipes. Supplying local restaurants and exporting to Singapore and Brunei.

businesses.name: Rungus Beadwork Studio
businesses.description: Authentic handmade Rungus beadwork (manik), traditional accessories, and wearable art. Each piece is crafted using centuries-old techniques passed down through generations.

businesses.name: Kinabalu Roots Eco Lodge
businesses.description: An immersive eco-lodge on the foothills of Mount Kinabalu offering cultural homestay experiences, guided highland treks, and traditional Dusun cuisine. A certified sustainable tourism operator.

businesses.name: Hinompuka Herbal Garden
businesses.description: A certified traditional medicine practice specializing in KDM herbal remedies including tuhau preparations, medicinal roots, and customized wellness consultations rooted in Kadazan ancestral knowledge.

businesses.name: Monsopiad Heritage Village
businesses.description: Sabah's premier cultural heritage site dedicated to the legendary Kadazan warrior Monsopiad. Offers guided tours, traditional dance shows, and a museum of indigenous artefacts.

businesses.name: Dusun Delights Catering
businesses.description: Specializing in authentic Dusun cuisine for weddings, corporate events, and Hari Kaamatan celebrations. Menu features hinava, tuhau dishes, bamboo-cooked rice, and pinasakan.

businesses.name: Tenom Agro-Tourism Farm
businesses.description: A working Murut farm offering agro-tourism packages including traditional paddy harvesting, indigenous crop tours, and farm-to-table dining experiences with Murut specialty dishes.

businesses.name: Kadazan Craft House
businesses.description: A curated gallery and retail shop featuring authentic KDM crafts from over 50 indigenous artisans. Stocks traditional costumes, bamboo instruments, hand-woven baskets, and ceremonial accessories.

businesses.name: Sabah KDM Language Academy
businesses.description: Offering conversational and literacy classes in Kadazan Dusun and Rungus languages. Also provides online courses, children's language programmes, and corporate cultural training.

businesses.name: Linangkit Cultural House
businesses.description: Dedicated to preserving and promoting the ancient Rungus weaving traditions. Visitors can watch master weavers at work, purchase authentic linangkit textiles, and book weaving workshops.

businesses.name: Highland KDM Organic Farm
businesses.description: Producer of certified organic highland vegetables, wild tuhau (wild ginger), and traditional ulam herbs. Supplies to KK restaurants and weekly farmers markets.

businesses.name: Bobohizan Cultural Wellness Centre
businesses.description: Offering traditional Kadazan healing practices guided by a certified bobohizan (high priestess). Services include ritual blessings, ancestral healing ceremonies, and cultural orientation retreats.

news.headline: Hari Kaamatan 2026: Record Turnout Expected at Kadamaian Festival Grounds
news.summary: Organizers of Sabah's biggest annual harvest festival anticipate over 80,000 visitors this year following the reopening of the expanded Kadamaian Festival Grounds in Penampang.

news.headline: New KDM Language Preservation Fund Launched with RM2 Million Government Grant
news.summary: The Ministry of National Unity has allocated RM2 million to establish a digital archive of Kadazan Dusun Murut Rungus oral histories and dialects, in partnership with Universiti Malaysia Sabah.

news.headline: Rungus Beadwork Recognized by UNESCO as Intangible Cultural Heritage
news.summary: The intricate manik beadwork tradition of the Rungus people of Kudat has been officially inscribed on UNESCO's Representative List of the Intangible Cultural Heritage of Humanity.

news.headline: KDM Youth Startup Wins National Innovation Award with AI Dialect Translator
news.summary: A team of Kadazan Dusun entrepreneurs from Kota Kinabalu has won the national MYStartup Innovation Grand Prix with an AI-powered mobile app that translates between 12 KDM dialects in real time.

news.headline: Sabah Govt Approves Indigenous Land Titles for 3,200 KDM Families in Ranau
news.summary: Following years of advocacy by community groups, the Sabah government has approved native customary rights (NCR) land titles for over 3,200 Kadazan Dusun families in the Ranau district.

news.headline: Mount Kinabalu International Folklore Festival Returns in June 2026
news.summary: The biennial folklore festival celebrating KDM and Bornean indigenous performing arts will return in June, featuring delegations from the Philippines, Indonesia, and Brunei alongside Sabahan groups.
